sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 55-year-old male, presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a skiing incident where he twisted his knee while landing from a jump on his boat on Lake Martin.
He reports the pain began immediately after the incident and has been worsening with activity.
The patient denies any hearing or feeling of something popping in his knee at the time of injury.
He has been managing his symptoms with over-the-counter Aleve and cold packs.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
He has a history of hypertension, for which he takes lisinopril 20mg daily, and has been managing his blood pressure well, with readings of 125/80 at home.
He denies any surgeries other than a tonsillectomy.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he takes lisinopril 20mg daily.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion present on his right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain with a knee effusion and inflammation.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; active; hypertension; active.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help reduce swelling and pain in the knee.
I ordered an X-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid profile.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the results of the X-ray are available, and I will re-evaluate the patient's knee.
